## [00:00 - 03:02]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer and Dramata didn't kill your ads Let's go So if you've spent any time in advertising circles lately You've probably heard it and drama to kill my results Now there's a lot of confusion and misinformation right now and honestly I get it. That's partly Metas fault But when you see that performance drops and things feel different The easiest explanation is to point at the newest shiny thing and say hey That's the reason it also helps when there's a whole lot of mystery about what that new shiny thing actually is But let's be clear about something the problem isn't andromeda The problem is that most advertisers don't even understand what andromeda is They've built this myth around it like it's a completely new algorithm out to destroy results It's not and really that gives andromeda way too much credit So this is where the confusion starts Andromeda at its core is this It's responsible for ad retrieval now repeat it to yourself if you have to and Andromeda is ad retrieval the upgrade to andromeda was necessary and the reason for that Was that there are countless? variations that advertisers can generate now from a single ad due to advantage plus creative enhancements text variations AI generated creative and more the prior Retrieval system couldn't handle all of those variations at scale it created a bottleneck So andromeda was a solution both for now and the future as more variations come with new enhancements and AI So yeah, andromeda is the new ad retrieval engine That's all it is it helps find the right ads for the right people and it does it more efficiently than the old system Now because advertisers misunderstand what andromeda is it leads to all Kinds of misinformation They blame it for their results and I see this kind of statement all the time Andromeda killed my results Spend some time on Reddit. You'll see it when you understand what andromeda is You realize this statement doesn't make any sense But they assume that because they were getting results before and they suddenly tank this year the correlation must mean causation That assumption requires believing andromeda is something different than it actually is

## [03:03 - 06:05]

But there are countless factors to impact the performance of your ads your strategy creative budget offer seasonality Competition but advertisers blame andromeda like it's the boogeyman They can't explain what it did wrong, but they're certain it's the problem and then the misunderstanding about andromeda Spills down to creative diversification Yeah, this is the newest buzz phrase that even meta is using over and over now Yes, andromeda can handle more creative variations more efficiently and yes That's why meta removed the old guidance about a six ad limit But some advertisers took that and created diversification To mean that they should create 25 or 50 ads Now while that might make sense for huge brands with creative teams and big budgets It's not a requirement and you won't get better results simply because you created more ads Creative diversification isn't about volume. It's about providing variety Quality options that allow meta to match the right message and format to the right person So that could mean images videos and a carousel Different aspect ratios Visually unique creative five primary text and headline options with meaningfully different angles Different creative for different customer types, but you don't have to do all of these things These are just the ways you can make creative more diverse and really it doesn't require a ton of ads You could create a single ad that reflects the spirit of creative diversification And this is something I'm doing right now So with one ad you could use different aspect ratios across placements Videos in some placements images and others you could use a 30 second 9 by 16 video and stories and reels And a longer version and feed use five text options five headlines even AI text variations edited of course to match your voice Use format display options to generate a carousel or collection related media AI backgrounds advantage plus creative enhancements do all of those things and you've created thousands of potential ad combinations all from one single ad Now I highly doubt that creating 25 or 50 ads Helps most small or medium-sized advertisers. It's a massive time commitment And it could be expensive and more often than not more ads means worse ads because if you need to create more What are you gonna do you're gonna cut corners? So here's the bottom of the glass Embrace in drama for what it is, but no more. It's the ad retrieval engine It can handle more creative variation, which means you can Diversify, but you don't need to drown yourself in work. There isn't a magic number of ads

## [06:06 - 07:53]

There isn't a secret method or hidden formula to please andromeda and it's not here to kill your results It's simply helping meta find the best creative faster So lean into creative diversification, but start small create one ad with true variety Built-in use text variations advantage plus creative smart enhancements Andromeda will not kill your results.
